summary: anthropic-toolkit postinstall harvests developer identity and cloud/git config to attacker endpoint
details: anthropic-toolkit@0.4.1 is a typosquat of the Anthropic SDK ecosystem (name 'anthropic-toolkit', description references the Anthropic SDK, peerDependency on @anthropic-ai/sdk). The declared main entry dist/index.js is absent from the tarball; the package's only payload is scripts/postinstall.js, which runs automatically on npm install. That script collects installer identity and credential-adjacent metadata from third-party tool configs the package does not own — hostname, username, OS user domain, cwd, parent package.json metadata, git user.email from ~/.gitconfig and repo.git/config, recent committer emails from git reflog, gh CLI identity from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, SSH public-key comments from ~/.ssh/*.pub, GCP account/project from ~/.config/gcloud/properties, AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config, and /etc/resolv.conf search domain — and POSTs the aggregated JSON to a hardcoded Google Cloud Run endpoint at npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. Banner comments framing the behavior as 'anonymous diagnostics' that 'never transmit credentials' contradict the implementation and do not constitute consent. The harvested identifiers enable account mapping, downstream targeting, and follow-on credential attacks against the developer's cloud and source-control identities.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
